Containers.Name: CI Runner Farm
Containers.Description: Turn your Unraid server into a fleet of GitHub Actions self-hosted BUILD runners — multiple concurrent, resource-capped runners running as Docker containers (no VM), with warm shared caches on a fast pool, queue-aware autoscaling, and Docker-in-Docker so jobs that use services: and docker build just work. [br][br] Point it at a repo or organization, paste a GitHub token, and your CI runs on your own hardware — as many jobs in parallel as your box can handle, with pnpm/npm/yarn/Playwright caches that stay hot between runs, at zero cost per minute. Everything is configured from a single webGUI page: store your token, Start/Stop/Restart/Scale, watch live status, and build your runner image. [br][br] [b]Security:[/b] self-hosted runners execute arbitrary workflow code on your hardware, and DinD runners run privileged. Use them only for trusted/private repositories — never let public/fork-PR code run on a privileged self-hosted runner. The plugin actively warns you when a privileged runner is pointed at a public repo. See the README for full guidance.
